meta.title: Gokazan Ski Area (Yubetsu) — LP Mock
meta.description: Drift ice × Lake Saroma × Yubetsu Drift Ice Pass. LP mock based on the Gokazan revenue-doubling strategy.

banner.text: Drift ice × Lake Saroma · Resort Pass strategy | Sawaguchi Group synergy | AI-generated mock images

hero.title:
Gokazan
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Overlook drift ice and Lake Saroma at once.

expert.title:
A local secret
for experts.
expert.lead: 79% advanced skiers. Okhotsk Course up to 27° with careful grooming and larch-protected snow—the opposite of Niseko, a local powder paradise.
expert.imgAlt: Carving on Okhotsk Course—a local expert hill where 79% are advanced

primary.title: Two seas below
primary.lead: Drift ice on the Okhotsk Sea and Japan's largest brackish Lake Saroma—overlook both at once, a globally rare location. When ice comes ashore, ski down toward a silver sea.
primary.imgAlt: Panorama overlooking Okhotsk drift ice and Lake Saroma together

secondary.title: Yubetsu Drift Ice Pass
secondary.lead: Move beyond a ¥1,500 lift-only day ticket. Combine onsen, dining, and skiing—triple spend per guest.

onsen.title:
After skiing,
Tulip-no-Yu.
onsen.lead: Ski area and Michi-no-Eki Kamiyubetsu Onsen Tulip-no-Yu—both managed by Sawaguchi Sangyo. Maximum synergy for fast packaging and shuttle networks.
onsen.imgAlt: Kamiyubetsu Onsen Tulip-no-Yu—warming bath after skiing

seafood.title:
Slope food,
reimagined as seafood.
seafood.lead: Scallops at Restaurant Ribbon, Ryugukan oysters—beyond ski-area curry clichés, Okhotsk seafood for high-spend visitors.
seafood.noticeBody: No permanent rentals. Weekend pop-up rental stations during drift-ice peak, or partner referrals on the web.
seafood.imgAlt: Yubetsu scallop dishes—premium seafood at Ribbon and more

tour.title:
Yubetsu winter
premium tour
tour.lead: From ¥1,500 point spending to line-and-area consumption—half- to full-day model courses to multiply ARPU.

am.title: Gokazan skiing
am.body: Ski drift-ice and Saroma views. XC too

noon.title: Yubetsu seafood
noon.body: Scallop & oyster lunch at Ribbon etc.

pm.title: Ryugudai Observatory
pm.body: Observe and photograph drift ice up close

drift.title: Drift ice × Japow guided tour
drift.body: Inbound B2B partnerships. Ungroomed runs + XC (snowshoe) + Yubetsu seafood dinner—¥30,000–50,000/person premium experiences linked from Shiretoko and Abashiri secondary transport.

passGuide.title: Gokazan Resort Pass guide
passGuide.body: Short-term action using Sawaguchi Sangyo's portfolio. ~3× spend per guest without new capital. Integrated pass: lift + onsen + dining coupon.

snowPlay.title: When you don't ski
snowPlay.body: Turn low-snow risk into opportunity. Snow BBQ, fire pits, sledding—monetize non-ski content without lifts. Complements families split by single-chair lifts.

access.line: 北海道紋別郡湧別町北兵村二区100番地
access.note: About 20 minutes by car from JR Engaru Station · about 5 minutes by car to Kamiyubetsu Onsen Tulip-no-Yu

footer.credit: Designated manager: Sawaguchi Sangyo Co., Ltd. · AI-generated mock images
